source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The turbine section in a jet engine receives gas (assume air) at \( 1200 \, \mathrm{K}, \, 800 \, \mathrm{kPa} \) with an ambient atmosphere at \( 80 \, \mathrm{kPa} \). The turbine is followed by a nozzle open to the atmosphere, and all the turbine work drives a compressor. What is the pressure at the nozzle inlet if all the turbine work is used to drive the compressor?

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate upstream labeling that the image is important
An image asset is present in image_manifest and registry_passed=true
The question text gives numeric states, but it does not fully spell out the full compressor-turbine-nozzle setup, so some context may come from the diagram
multi_step: The target quantity is inferred rather than directly stated: pressure at the nozzle inlet must be derived from the given thermodynamic conditions
The prompt combines multiple conditions, including turbine inlet state, ambient pressure, nozzle open to atmosphere, and the constraint that all turbine work drives a compressor
This is a quantitative engineering/physics reasoning task with several given values rather than a single-step retrieval
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and answer_text is a specific numeric pressure value with unit
choices are provided, so the final answer can also be checked against a discrete option set
registry_passed=true and answer_present=true, which supports reliable downstream checking

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present with registry_passed=true and no recorded asset issues
high verifiability score with a specific short-text numeric answer and option-based checking support
image-dependent context appears recoverable because the referenced image asset is available
multi-step engineering reasoning value is reasonably strong, supporting inclusion in the candidate pool
noted risks are moderate follow-up concerns rather than core semantic gaps
